title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Risk Brief: Done Contract
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates done contract increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Done Contract evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-done-contract-gro6p: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Done Contract into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-done-contract-gro6p:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
